questions.text: Which of the following substances exhibits hydrogen bonding?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonding occurs in those substances in which a highly electronegative atom (such as Fluorine (F), Oxygen (O) or Nitrogen (N)) is directly bonded to Hydrogen. The presence of one of these three atoms is a REQUIREMENT for hydrogen bonding to occur. Only option C mentions a substance which has one of these three atoms, i.e. Nitrogen in NH3, while the other options mention other atoms aside from the three highly electronegative species mentioned previously.

questions.text: The shape of CO2 molecule is similar to:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: CO2 has a linear structure with a bond angle of 180 degrees with the central atom (C) having no lone pairs and being sp hybrid. Beryllium in BeF2 has the same configuration. Other options are wrong because: H2S= sp3 SO2=sp2 SnCl2=sp2

questions.text: Both ionic and covalent bonds are present in:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Only covalent bonds are present in methane (CH4). Methane is a covalent molecule composed of one carbon atom and four hydrogen atoms.
In SO2 (sulfur dioxide), the sulfur atom forms a covalent bond with two oxygen atoms.

Potassium chloride (KCl) contains an ionic bond between the potassium cation (K+) and chloride anion (Cl-). The two ions are held together by strong electrostatic forces due to their opposite charges.

Sodium hydroxide (NaOH) contains both covalent and ionic bonds. The sodium ion (Na+) and hydroxide ion (OH-) are held together by an ionic bond, while the covalent bond is between the hydrogen and oxygen atoms in the hydroxide ion(-OH).

questions.explanation: Sodium hydroxide (NaOH) contains both covalent and ionic bonds. The sodium ion (Na+) and hydroxide ion (OH-) are held together by an ionic bond, while the covalent bond is between the hydrogen and oxygen atoms in the hydroxide ion(-OH).

questions.text: Which of the following molecules has the largest number of shared pairs of electrons?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: C2H4 has the largest number of shared electrons as it has 12 valence electrons in total which are all part of a shared pair (4 electrons between the two central carbons and 2 electrons each between 4 carbon-Hydrogen bonds). N2 has 3 shared pairs of electron (6 electrons in total) CO2 has 4 shared pairs of electrons (8 electrons in total). NH3 has 3 shared pairs of electrons. (6 electrons in total)

questions.text: The stability in the following structure is due to the:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Van der Waal's forces are weak interactions that are not sufficient to stabilize the specific structure in question.
Disulfide bridges are covalent bonds that stabilize tertiary structures, not typically involved in the stabilization of the structure described in the question.

options.text: Presence of unpaired electron in the structure
options.explanation: Unpaired electrons can lead to reactive species, which do not contribute to stability. This is irrelevant to the stability of the described structure.

options.text: Hydrogen bonding between NH group of one peptide and CO group of another peptide
options.explanation: Hydrogen bonding between the NH group of one peptide bond and the CO group of another stabilizes the alpha-helix structure in proteins.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that the stability in the structure is due to hydrogen bonding between the NH group of one peptide and the CO group of another peptide. This type of hydrogen bonding is characteristic of the alpha-helix structure in proteins, which is a common secondary structural motif. The backbone N−H group hydrogen bonds to the backbone C=O group of an amino acid located three or four residues earlier along the protein sequence, stabilizing the helical structure.Option A, Weak van der Waal’s forces, are generally too weak to provide significant stabilization in this context. Option B, Disulfide bridges, are not involved in secondary structure stabilization but rather in tertiary structure. Option C, Presence of unpaired electron in the structure, is unrelated to the stabilization of protein structures.

questions.text: Which one of the following molecules has sp3 hybridization?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: CH4 has sp3 hybridization which enables all four bonds to be of the same length and strength. Methane molecule is formed by the overlap of sp3 hybrid orbitals of carbon with 1s orbitals of four hydrogen atoms separately to form four sigma bonds.
Option B, C2H4 has sp2 hybridization as carbon atom contains three single bonds which include sp2 orbitals, whereas the C-C double bond is brought about by a p-p overlap.

Options, C and D, both have sp hybridization. In CO2, carbon has two single bonds which involve sp orbitals, and a double bond with each oxygen so, two free p orbitals are used for the two double bonds. In C2H2, an alkyne, carbon has two single bonds which involve sp orbitals, but a triple bond that uses two p orbitals per carbon atom.

Options, C and D, both have sp hybridization. In CO2, carbon has two single bonds which involve sp orbitals, and a double bond with each oxygen so, two free p orbitals are used for the two double bonds. In C2H2, an alkyne, carbon has two single bonds which involve sp orbitals, but a triple bond that uses two p orbitals per carbon atom.

questions.explanation: CH4 has sp3 hybridization which enables all four bonds to be of the same length and strength. Methane molecule is formed by the overlap of sp3 hybrid orbitals of carbon with 1s orbitals of four hydrogen atoms separately to form four sigma bonds.

questions.text: Which of the following indicate the correct variation of electronegativity?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It is incorrect because nitrogen has a higher electronegativity than oxygen.
This option is incorrect because nitrogen has an electronegativity less than oxygen.
This option is incorrect because the electronegativity order is shown as completely opposite.

The relative tendency of a bonded atom in a molecule to attract the shared electron pair towards itself is called electronegativity. The positively charged protons in the nucleus attract the negatively charged electrons. As the number of protons in the nucleus increases, the electronegativity or attraction will increase. Therefore electronegativity increases from left to right in a row in the periodic table. Here, the correct order of electronegativity is: F > O > N > C

questions.explanation: The relative tendency of a bonded atom in a molecule to attract the shared electron pair towards itself is called electronegativity. The positively charged protons in the nucleus attract the negatively charged electrons. As the number of protons in the nucleus increases, the electronegativity or attraction will increase. Therefore electronegativity increases from left to right in a row in the periodic table. Here, the correct order of electronegativity is: F > O > N > C

questions.text: An ionic compound is most likely to be formed when:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Ionization energy of A is high but electron affinity of B is low
options.explanation: This is not correct as in ionic compound general represention is A is metal Cation and B is non-metal anion, so metal will have low ionization energy to be able to lose an electron and similarly non metals will have high electron affinity to be able to gain electron.

options.text: The ionization energy of A is low but electron affinity of B is high
options.explanation: The option "The ionization energy of A is low but electron affinity of B is high" is most likely to form an ionic compound. In an ionic compound, one element has a low ionization energy, which means it readily gives up electrons to form a cation, while the other element has a high electron affinity, which means it readily accepts electrons to form an anion. This results in the formation of an ionic bond between the cation and anion, forming an ionic compound.

options.text: Both ionization energy of A and electron affinity of B are high
options.explanation: The option "The ionization energy of A is low but electron affinity of B is high" is most likely to form an ionic compound. In an ionic compound, one element has a low ionization energy, which means it readily gives up electrons to form a cation, while the other element has a high electron affinity, which means it readily accepts electrons to form an anion. This results in the formation of an ionic bond between the cation and anion, forming an ionic compound.

options.text: Both ionization energy of A and electron affinity of B are low
options.explanation: The option "The ionization energy of A is low but electron affinity of B is high" is most likely to form an ionic compound. In an ionic compound, one element has a low ionization energy, which means it readily gives up electrons to form a cation, while the other element has a high electron affinity, which means it readily accepts electrons to form an anion. This results in the formation of an ionic bond between the cation and anion, forming an ionic compound.

questions.explanation: The option "The ionization energy of A is low but electron affinity of B is high" is most likely to form an ionic compound. In an ionic compound, one element has a low ionization energy, which means it readily gives up electrons to form a cation, while the other element has a high electron affinity, which means it readily accepts electrons to form an anion. This results in the formation of an ionic bond between the cation and anion, forming an ionic compound.

questions.text: Sigma bond is formed by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because the transfer of electrons leads to the formation of ionic bonds, not sigma bonds.
Option B is correct. A sigma bond is characterized by the head-on overlapping of atomic orbitals, allowing for the strongest type of covalent bond.

options.text: Mutual but unequal sharing of electrons
options.explanation: Option C is incorrect because mutual but unequal sharing of electrons results in a polar covalent bond, not a sigma bond.
Option D is incorrect because parallel overlapping of atomic orbitals results in the formation of pi bonds, not sigma bonds.
Option E is incorrect since only option B describes the correct process for forming sigma bonds.

questions.explanation: The sigma bond, the strongest type of covalent bond, is formed by the head-on overlapping of atomic orbitals. This overlap allows for maximum electron density between the two bonding nuclei, resulting in a stable bond. In contrast, ionic bonds are formed by the transfer of electrons (Option A), polar covalent bonds arise from mutual but unequal sharing of electrons (Option C), and pi bonds result from the parallel overlapping of atomic orbitals (Option D). Thus, Option B is the only correct description for sigma bond formation.

questions.text: The equation shows the reaction between element X and dilute hydrochloric acid: X(s) + 2HCl(aq) -> XCl2(aq) + H2(g) What types of bonding is present in element X and in compound XCl2?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It is incorrect as the compound X has metallic bonds, whereas the compound XCl2 has ionic bonds.
It is incorrect as the compound X has metallic bonds, whereas the compound XCl2 has ionic bonds.
It is incorrect as the compound X has metallic bonds, where as the compound XCl2 has ionic bonds.

Metallic bonding is a type of chemical bonding that arises from the electrostatic attractive force between conduction electrons (in the form of an electron cloud of delocalized electrons) and positively charged metal ions. The element X is metal as Metals react with halogens to form salts.The compound XCl2 is a salt and has ionic bonds. As two chlorine atoms are attached, this shows the central metal has two valence electrons in free states, so it is a metal of group II A[Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba].

questions.explanation: Metallic bonding is a type of chemical bonding that arises from the electrostatic attractive force between conduction electrons (in the form of an electron cloud of delocalized electrons) and positively charged metal ions. The element X is metal as Metals react with halogens to form salts.The compound XCl2 is a salt and has ionic bonds. As two chlorine atoms are attached, this shows the central metal has two valence electrons in free states, so it is a metal of group II A[Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba].

questions.text: Which of the following molecules have zero Dipole Moment?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The CCl4 molecule has zero dipole moment. Despite the polar C-Cl bonds, the symmetrical tetrahedral shape causes the dipoles to cancel each other out.
CO2 is a linear molecule. The bond dipole moments in the opposing C=O bonds cancel each other, resulting in a zero dipole moment.
Cl2 consists of two identical atoms, resulting in no net dipole moment due to the absence of a charge difference.
Benzene (C6H6) has a symmetrical planar structure. The dipole moments of the C-H bonds cancel out, leading to a zero net dipole moment.
All the molecules listed have zero dipole moment due to their symmetrical geometries, which cause the dipole moments to cancel out.

questions.explanation: All the molecules listed have a zero dipole moment due to their symmetrical structures:CCl4: Although it has polar C-Cl bonds, the tetrahedral symmetry results in a net dipole moment of zero.CO2: The linear shape with two opposing C=O bonds results in the cancellation of dipole moments.Cl2: As it consists of identical atoms, no dipole moment arises.C6H6 (Benzene): The planar symmetrical structure leads to the cancellation of dipole moments of C-H bonds.Therefore, the correct answer is 'All of the above'.

questions.text: Bond energy:I. is the energy required to break a bond between two atoms in a diatomic molecule II. Is taken as the energy released in forming a bond form free atoms III. Is the measure of the strength of bond.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: While option I correctly describes bond energy, it is not the only valid description. Options II and III also describe bond energy.
Options I and II both describe bond energy, but option III is also a valid description as it relates to the strength of a bond.
Options I and III describe bond energy, but option II is also a necessary component as it involves the energy released during bond formation.
While option II describes bond energy in terms of energy released, options I and III are also correct descriptions.

All three statements correctly describe bond energy. Bond energy is the energy required to break a bond and is equivalent to the energy released when the bond is formed. It also indicates the strength of the bond.

questions.explanation: Bond energy is a critical concept in understanding chemical bonding and is defined through multiple perspectives:Option I: It is indeed the energy required to break a bond between two atoms in a diatomic molecule, indicating how much energy is needed to overcome the bond's strength.Option II: The energy released when a bond is formed from free atoms is also a measure of bond energy, showing the stabilization energy provided during bond formation.Option III: Bond energy serves as an indicator of bond strength; stronger bonds require more energy to break, thus having higher bond energies.Therefore, all three options collectively provide a comprehensive understanding of bond energy, making Option E the correct choice. Each statement highlights a different but related aspect of bond energy, and understanding this concept requires integrating all perspectives.

questions.text: The boiling point of water is higher than other hydrides because water molecules can form:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Each water (H₂O) molecule can form four hydrogen bonds in total:2 hydrogen atoms → can each form 1 hydrogen bond (as donors)2 lone pairs on oxygen → can each form 1 hydrogen bond (as acceptors)Total = 4 hydrogen bonds per water molecule

Each water (H₂O) molecule can form four hydrogen bonds in total:2 hydrogen atoms → can each form 1 hydrogen bond (as donors)2 lone pairs on oxygen → can each form 1 hydrogen bond (as acceptors)Total = 4 hydrogen bonds per water molecule

Each water (H₂O) molecule can form four hydrogen bonds in total:2 hydrogen atoms → can each form 1 hydrogen bond (as donors)2 lone pairs on oxygen → can each form 1 hydrogen bond (as acceptors)Total = 4 hydrogen bonds per water molecule

Each water (H₂O) molecule can form four hydrogen bonds in total:2 hydrogen atoms → can each form 1 hydrogen bond (as donors)2 lone pairs on oxygen → can each form 1 hydrogen bond (as acceptors)Total = 4 hydrogen bonds per water molecule

questions.explanation: Each water (H₂O) molecule can form four hydrogen bonds in total:2 hydrogen atoms → can each form 1 hydrogen bond (as donors)2 lone pairs on oxygen → can each form 1 hydrogen bond (as acceptors)Total = 4 hydrogen bonds per water molecule

questions.text: The number of bond(s) between carbon and nitrogen atoms in a Nitrile is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Carbon atom forms triple bonds with nitrogen atom, hence incorrect
Carbon forms one sigma and two pi-bonds with nitrogen, hence this answer is incorrect
Carbon does not only form 1 sigma bond with nitrogen, it is also accompanied by 2 pie bonds, hence it is incorrect

Both the carbon and the nitrogen are sp hydridized which leaves them both with two p orbitals which overlap to form the two π bond in the triple bond. The bond angle in nitrile is 180° which gives a nitrile functional group a linear shape.

questions.explanation: Both the carbon and the nitrogen are sp hydridized which leaves them both with two p orbitals which overlap to form the two π bond in the triple bond. The bond angle in nitrile is 180° which give a nitrile functional group a linear shape.

questions.text: If the difference between electronegativity values between two atoms is less than 1.7 , the bond is necessarily:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A bond is considered ionic if the electronegativity difference is greater than 1.7. Since the difference here is less than 1.7, it is not ionic.
When the electronegativity difference is less than 1.7, the bond is typically covalent. This is the case in this scenario, making the bond covalent.
Electrovalent is another term for ionic bonds, which occur when the electronegativity difference is greater than 1.7, not less.
A polar covalent bond occurs when the electronegativity difference is between 0.5 and 1.7. While this could be true, it does not specifically address whether the bond is covalent or ionic.
Non-polar covalent bonds occur when the electronegativity difference is very small, typically less than 0.5. This does not address the covalent nature of a bond with a difference less than 1.7.

questions.explanation: If the electronegative difference is >1.7 then its nature is ionic and <1.7 then its nature is covalent and if its equal to 1.7 then nature of bond will be 50% ionic and 50% covalent. As here, it is less than 1.7, nature is covalent.

questions.text: The geometry of H2S and its dipole moment are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: H2S has a bent geometry due to its 2 lone pairs and 2 bond pairs. The molecule is slightly polar because sulfur is more electronegative than hydrogen, leading to a non-zero dipole moment. Therefore, the geometry is angular, and the dipole moment is non-zero.
This is incorrect because, despite the angular geometry, the difference in electronegativity between sulfur and hydrogen results in a non-zero dipole moment.
Linear geometry is incorrect for H2S due to the presence of lone pairs, which push the hydrogen atoms closer together, forming a bent shape. Furthermore, the dipole moment is non-zero.
Linear geometry is incorrect for H2S. The molecule's geometry is angular due to the lone pairs, and while the dipole moment is non-zero, the incorrect geometry makes this option invalid.

questions.explanation: H2S has a bent geometry according to VSEPR. It has 2 lone pairs and 2 bond pairs and a bond angle of 92o degrees. The dipole moment is non-zero because sulfur is more electronegative than hydrogen and makes the molecule slightly polar. Thus, the geometry of H2S and its dipole moment are Angular and non-zero.

questions.text: Which of the following has the highest value of electronegativity?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.
The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.
The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.

Elements from the halogen group including F, Cl, Br have pretty high electronegativities. The most electronegative element is Fluorine with a score of 4.0 (the highest possible.) Across from Fluorine we also have N and O with high electronegativities. Electronegativity is basically how much the elements 'want' the electrons. A simple way to think about it is that the closer an element is to Fluorine, the higher its electronegativity. The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.

questions.explanation: Elements from the halogen group including F, Cl, Br have pretty high electronegativities. The most electronegative element is Fluorine with a score of 4.0 (the highest possible.) Across from Fluorine we also have N and O with high electronegativities. Electronegativity is basically how much the elements 'want' the electrons. A simple way to think about it is that the closer an element is to Fluorine, the higher its electronegativity. The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.

questions.text: Which of the following hybrid orbitals has maximum “s” character?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Expressions shown in the options e.g. sp2 basically mean that an s orbital is merged with 2 p orbitals. The number in the subscript of p represents the number of p orbitals involved in the hybridization. The greater this number is, the less would be the characteristics of the s orbital apparent in the hybrid orbitals. There is no subscript in sp hybridization hence, it has the highest s-character among all hybridizations.

The greater this number is, the less would be the characteristics of the s orbital apparent in the hybrid orbitals. There is no subscript in sp hybridization hence, it has the highest s-character among all hybridizations.

The greater this number is, the less would be the characteristics of the s orbital apparent in the hybrid orbitals. There is no subscript in sp hybridization hence, it has the highest s-character among all hybridizations.

The number in the subscript of p represents the number of p orbitals involved in the hybridization. The greater this number is, the less would be the characteristics of the s orbital apparent in the hybrid orbitals. There is no subscript in sp hybridization hence, it has the highest s-character among all hybridizations.

questions.text: The order of force of repulsion between Lone pairs and Bond pairs is?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Lone pair - Bond pair repulsion > Lone pair - Lone pair repulsion > Bond pair - Bond pair repulsion
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because lone pair - lone pair repulsion is stronger than lone pair - bond pair repulsion due to the lack of attraction to nuclei in lone pairs.

options.text: Bond pair - Bond pair repulsion > Lone pair - Lone pair repulsion > Lone pair - Bond pair repulsion
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because bond pair - bond pair repulsion is the weakest due to the attraction between bonding electrons and nuclei.

options.text: Lone pair - Lone pair repulsion > Bond pair - Bond pair repulsion > Lone pair - Bond pair repulsion
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because lone pair - bond pair repulsion is stronger than bond pair - bond pair repulsion due to the presence of non-bonding electron pairs.

options.text: Lone pair - Lone pair repulsion > Lone pair - Bond pair repulsion > Bond pair - Bond pair repulsion
options.explanation: This option is correct because lone pair - lone pair repulsion is the strongest due to the lack of attraction to nuclei, followed by lone pair - bond pair repulsion, and then bond pair - bond pair repulsion is the weakest due to the stabilizing effect of bonding electrons with nuclei.

questions.explanation: The correct order of repulsions is: Lone pair - Lone pair repulsion > Lone pair - Bond pair repulsion > Bond pair - Bond pair repulsion. This is because lone pairs of electrons are not shared between atoms and are therefore more concentrated and closer to the central atom, leading to greater repulsion. Lone pair - bond pair repulsion is less than lone pair - lone pair but more than bond pair - bond pair, as the presence of a lone pair introduces more electron density compared to the shared electron density in bond pairs. Bond pair - bond pair repulsion is the weakest due to the attraction between the electrons and their respective nuclei, which stabilizes the electrons in the bond.

questions.text: H2O has higher boiling point than HF because?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.text: H2O can form more hydrogen bonds
options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Water (H2O) has a higher boiling point than hydrogen fluoride (HF) because, despite both forming hydrogen bonds, water's tetrahedral shape allows it to form twice as many hydrogen bonds per molecule, requiring more energy to break.

While it's true that HF has stronger individual hydrogen bonds due to the higher electronegativity of fluorine, the number of hydrogen bonds formed per molecule is more significant in determining the overall boiling point. H2O can form more hydrogen bonds, which outweighs the strength of individual HF hydrogen bonds.

options.text: H2O has a higher molecular weight.
options.explanation: This is not the primary reason for the higher boiling point of H2O. While molecular weight can influence boiling point to some extent, the effect of hydrogen bonding is more significant in this case.
Both H2O and HF are polar molecules. Polarity contributes to intermolecular forces, but in this case, the number and strength of hydrogen bonds are the dominant factors determining the boiling point.

questions.explanation: Water (H2O) has a higher boiling point than hydrogen fluoride (HF) because, despite both forming hydrogen bonds, water's tetrahedral shape allows it to form twice as many hydrogen bonds per molecule, requiring more energy to break.

questions.text: Ionic, covalent and co-ordinate covalent bonds are simultaneously present in the molecular geometry of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ammonia has a nitrogen atom bound with three hydrogen atoms by covalent bonds.
There are 3 covalent , 1 coordinate covalent bond in NH4+ ion, and 1 covalent bond in OH−. There is an ionic bond between NH4+ and OH-.So, this species simultaneously has 3 different types of bonds.
Hydrochloric acid has ionic bonding between Hydrogen (H+) and chlorine(Cl-).
Water has only covalent bonding between 2 hydrogen atoms and an oxygen atom.
Methane also has 4 covalent bonds by which carbon atoms are bonded to 4 respective hydrogen atoms.

questions.explanation: There are 3 covalent , 1 coordinate covalent bond in NH4+ ion, and 1 covalent bond in OH−. There is an ionic bond between NH4+ and OH-.So, this species simultaneously has 3 different types of bonds.

questions.text: The substance which conducts electricity by the movement of ions:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Graphite, copper, mercury conduct electricity because they have mobile electrons which move around and conduct electricity.
Graphite, copper, mercury conduct electricity because they have mobile electrons which move around and conduct electricity.

In the solid state, ionic compounds such as sodium chloride have their ions fixed in position and therefore these ions cannot move so solid ionic compounds cannot conduct electricity. However in the molten state, ions in ionic compounds are free to flow and therefore molten sodium chloride can conduct electricity. Molten NaCl has mobile Na+ and Cl- ions which can move and conduct electricity.
Graphite, copper, mercury conduct electricity because they have mobile electrons which move around and conduct electricity.

questions.explanation: In the solid state, ionic compounds such as sodium chloride have their ions fixed in position and therefore these ions cannot move so solid ionic compounds cannot conduct electricity. However in the molten state, ions in ionic compounds are free to flow and therefore molten sodium chloride can conduct electricity. Molten NaCl has mobile Na+ and Cl- ions which can move and conduct electricity.

questions.text: Which pair has species with different shapes?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In BeCl2 (beryllium chloride), the central atom (Be) is bonded to two chlorine atoms, resulting in a linear molecular geometry.In CO2 (carbon dioxide), the central atom (C) is bonded to two oxygen atoms, also leading to a linear molecular geometry. Both have the same shape.

In NH3 (ammonia), the central nitrogen atom is bonded to three hydrogen atoms with a lone pair, forming a trigonal pyramidal shape.In BF3 (boron trifluoride), the central boron atom is bonded to three fluorine atoms, forming a trigonal planar shape. These species have different shapes.

In SCl2 (sulfur dichloride), the central atom (S) is bonded to two chlorine atoms, resulting in a bent or angular shape due to lone pairs.In H2O (water), the central oxygen atom is bonded to two hydrogen atoms, also forming a bent or angular shape. Both have the same shape.

In CH4 (methane), the central carbon atom is bonded to four hydrogen atoms, forming a tetrahedral shape.In NH4+ (ammonium ion), the central nitrogen atom is bonded to four hydrogen atoms, also forming a tetrahedral shape. Both have the same shape.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option B: NH3 and BF3. According to VSEPR theory, NH3 has a trigonal pyramidal shape due to the lone pair on the nitrogen atom, while BF3 has a trigonal planar shape with no lone pairs on the boron atom, resulting in different geometries for these two molecules.Option A: BeCl2 and CO2 both have a linear shape due to their bonding arrangements with no lone pairs affecting the geometry.Option C: SCl2 and H2O both have a bent or angular shape due to the presence of lone pairs on the central atom, leading to similar geometries.Option D: CH4 and NH4+ both have a tetrahedral shape, as each central atom is bonded to four atoms with no lone pairs, resulting in identical geometries.

questions.text: In HF the covalent bond is due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option suggests the overlap of two s orbitals, which is not applicable to the HF bond. HF involves an s orbital from hydrogen and a p orbital from fluorine.

In the formation of HF, the 1s orbital of hydrogen overlaps with the 2Pz orbital of fluorine to form a covalent bond. The electron density from both atoms is concentrated along the bond axis, making this the correct description of the overlap.
This option suggests overlap between an s orbital and a Px orbital, which is not the case in HF. The correct overlap involves the Pz orbital of fluorine.
This option describes overlap between two Pz orbitals, which does not occur in HF. The bond involves an overlap between an s orbital from hydrogen and a Pz orbital from fluorine.

questions.explanation: In formation of HF, 1s orbital of H overlaps with 2Pz orbital to F to form bond. Therefore bonding is due to S-Pz overlapping.

questions.text: The number of sigma and pi bonds in 1-butene-3-yne?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The number of sigma and pi-bonds in 1-butene 3-yne are 7 sigma and 3 pi. It has one C=C double bond which contains one pi bond. It has one C≡C triple bond which contains two pi bonds. It has 3 carbon carbon sigma bonds and 4 C−H pi bonds.

The number of sigma and pi-bonds in 1-butene 3-yne are 7 sigma and 3 pi. It has one C=C double bond which contains one pi bond. It has one C≡C triple bond which contains two pi bonds. It has 3 carbon carbon sigma bonds and 4 C−H pi bonds.

The number of sigma and pi-bonds in 1-butene 3-yne are 7 sigma and 3 pi. It has one C=C double bond which contains one pi bond. It has one C≡C triple bond which contains two pi bonds. It has 3 carbon carbon sigma bonds and 4 C−H pi bonds.

The number of sigma and pi-bonds in 1-butene 3-yne are 7 sigma and 3 pi. It has one C=C double bond which contains one pi bond. It has one C≡C triple bond which contains two pi bonds. It has 3 carbon carbon sigma bonds and 4 C−H pi bonds.

questions.explanation: The number of sigma and pi-bonds in 1-butene 3-yne are 7 sigma and 3 pi. It has one C=C double bond which contains one pi bond. It has one C≡C triple bond which contains two pi bonds. It has 3 carbon carbon sigma bonds and 4 C−H pi bonds.

questions.text: The shape of SnCl2 is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Linear: A linear shape would require the two chlorine atoms to be 180 degrees apart, which would only happen if there were no lone pair on tin. The lone pair's presence forces the molecule to bend.
Tetrahedral: A tetrahedral shape requires four electron groups around the central atom. SnCl2 only has three (two bonds, one lone pair).

SnCl2 (Tin dichloride) has a bent or V-shaped geometry, which is an example of an angular or non-linear shape. The molecule has a central tin atom that is bonded to two chlorine atoms. The two bond pairs of electrons repel each other, resulting in a bent shape. The bond angle is approximately 119 degrees. This bent shape is due to the lone pair of electrons present on the tin atom. The lone pair occupies a greater amount of space compared to the bond pair, resulting in the distortion of the molecule. In contrast, the linear shape is a molecular geometry in which the atoms of the molecule are arranged in a straight line, like in HCl or CO2. Tetrahedral shape is a molecular geometry that has four atoms attached to a central atom, like in CH4 or CCl4. Trigonal shape is a molecular geometry that has three atoms attached to a central atom, like in BF3.

Trigonal Planar: While the electron group geometry is based on trigonal planar, the molecular shape is different due to the lone pair's influence. If the lone pair's repulsion didn't alter the shape, then trigonal planar would be correct. However, it does, making the molecule bent.

questions.explanation: SnCl2 (Tin dichloride) has a bent or V-shaped geometry, which is an example of an angular or non-linear shape. The molecule has a central tin atom that is bonded to two chlorine atoms. The two bond pairs of electrons repel each other, resulting in a bent shape. The bond angle is approximately 119 degrees. This bent shape is due to the lone pair of electrons present on the tin atom. The lone pair occupies a greater amount of space compared to the bond pair, resulting in the distortion of the molecule. In contrast, the linear shape is a molecular geometry in which the atoms of the molecule are arranged in a straight line, like in HCl or CO2. Tetrahedral shape is a molecular geometry that has four atoms attached to a central atom, like in CH4 or CCl4. Trigonal shape is a molecular geometry that has three atoms attached to a central atom, like in BF3.

questions.text: Which of the following Halogens molecules has maximum bond energy?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: We would expect the B.E of F-F to be the highest but this isn’t so. The reason is that the fluorine atom having the highest electronegativity is smaller in size than the other atoms of its group. Due to this the inter-nuclear repulsive forces operating are larger than others and with a small amount of energy, the bond can be broken down.

Bond energy( B.E ) is a measure of the bond strength of a chemical bond, and is the amount of energy needed to break the atoms involved in a molecular bond into free atoms. B.E. depends on the atomic radius. Going down the group, in halogens, the bond energy decreases. It is because down the group the distance between the outermost electrons and nucleus decreases, decreasing the force of attraction between them. There is an exception in the halogen group which is of fluorine. This is because the atomic radius of fluorine atoms is so small that when two fluorine atoms form an F-F bond, the internuclear repulsive forces between them reduces the bond strength and thus the bond energy. That is why despite being on the top-most position, fluorine has lower bond energy. Where Chlorine is having the least radius thus, its B.E is the highest among all the members of its group.
Bromine’s radius is larger than Cl. As a result, its B.E. is also smaller than Cl. And this option is incorrect.
Iodine’s radius is even larger than bromine, thus having an even smaller B.E than bromine and being the incorrect option.

questions.explanation: Bond energy( B.E ) is a measure of the bond strength of a chemical bond, and is the amount of energy needed to break the atoms involved in a molecular bond into free atoms. B.E. depends on the atomic radius. Going down the group, in halogens, the bond energy decreases. It is because down the group the distance between the outermost electrons and nucleus decreases, decreasing the force of attraction between them. There is an exception in the halogen group which is of fluorine. This is because the atomic radius of fluorine atoms is so small that when two fluorine atoms form an F-F bond, the internuclear repulsive forces between them reduces the bond strength and thus the bond energy. That is why despite being on the top-most position, fluorine has lower bond energy. Where Chlorine is having the least radius thus, its B.E is the highest among all the members of its group.

questions.text: For the formation of ionic bond, electronegativity difference should be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Zero electronegativity difference results in the formation of non-polar covalent bond.
0.5 electronegativity difference also results in the formation of non-polar covalent bond.

Electronegativity is a measure of an atom's ability to attract electrons towards itself in a chemical bond. For the formation of an ionic bond, there should be a significant difference in electronegativity between the participating atoms. Typically, an electronegativity difference of 1.7 or greater on the Pauling scale is considered indicative of an ionic bond. In other words, if the electronegativity difference between two atoms is equal to or greater than 1.7, it suggests that one atom has a much stronger attraction for electrons than the other.
Less than 1.7 electronegativity difference results in the formation of polar covalent bond.

questions.explanation: Electronegativity is a measure of an atom's ability to attract electrons towards itself in a chemical bond. For the formation of an ionic bond, there should be a significant difference in electronegativity between the participating atoms. Typically, an electronegativity difference of 1.7 or greater on the Pauling scale is considered indicative of an ionic bond. In other words, if the electronegativity difference between two atoms is equal to or greater than 1.7, it suggests that one atom has a much stronger attraction for electrons than the other.

questions.text: Linear shaped hybrid orbital is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Linear-shaped hybrid orbitals are formed by the combination of one s orbital and one p orbital. The resulting hybrid orbital is called an sp orbital. The angle between the two orbitals is 180°. Examples of molecules that contain sp hybrid orbitals include acetylene (C2H2) and carbon monoxide (CO).

The hybrid orbital designated as "sp2" refers to a trigonal planar-shaped hybrid orbital. It is formed when one s orbital and two p orbitals from the same atom combine to form three hybrid orbitals. The sp2 hybridization occurs when an atom is involved in three sigma bonds and has one lone pair of electrons. The resulting trigonal planar geometry allows for a planar arrangement of bonding and non-bonding electron pairs around the central atom.

The hybrid orbital designated as "sp3" refers to a tetrahedral-shaped hybrid orbital. It is formed when one s orbital and three p orbitals from the same atom combine to form four hybrid orbitals. The sp3 hybridization occurs when an atom is involved in four sigma bonds and has no lone pairs of electrons. The resulting tetrahedral geometry allows for a tetrahedral arrangement of bonding and non-bonding electron pairs around the central atom.
The designation "dsp" is not commonly used to describe hybrid orbitals. It does not represent a specific hybridization state or shape of orbitals.

questions.explanation: Linear-shaped hybrid orbitals are formed by the combination of one s orbital and one p orbital. The resulting hybrid orbital is called an sp orbital. The angle between the two orbitals is 180°. Examples of molecules that contain sp hybrid orbitals include acetylene (C2H2) and carbon monoxide (CO).

questions.text: Which of the following is tetrahedral?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This angle does not correspond to a tetrahedral geometry as it represents a linear arrangement of atoms, not a tetrahedral shape.

A tetrahedral arrangement has four atoms or groups bonded to a central atom, creating a three-dimensional shape with bond angles of approximately 109.5 degrees. This geometry is commonly found in molecules with four bonding pairs of electrons and no lone pairs, such as methane (CH4) or carbon tetrachloride (CCl4).
This angle corresponds to a trigonal planar geometry, not a tetrahedral geometry.
This angle corresponds to a square planar or square pyramidal geometry, not a tetrahedral geometry.

questions.explanation: A tetrahedral arrangement has four atoms or groups bonded to a central atom, creating a three-dimensional shape with bond angles of approximately 109.5 degrees. This geometry is commonly found in molecules with four bonding pairs of electrons and no lone pairs, such as methane (CH4) or carbon tetrachloride (CCl4).

questions.text: The bond formed b/w NH3and BF3is co-ordinate covalent bond because NH3 is_ due to presence of one lone pair:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An acceptor species is an atom or molecule that can accept electrons. In the context of NH3 and BF3, boron in BF3 acts as the acceptor because it has an empty orbital to accept electrons. NH3, however, does not act as the acceptor in this bond.

A donor species is a molecule or ion that donates a pair of electrons to form a bond. In the bond between NH3 and BF3, NH3 acts as the donor because the nitrogen atom donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3, forming a coordinate covalent bond.
A neutral species is one that has no net charge. While NH3 is neutral overall, this option does not address the role of NH3 as a donor in the formation of a coordinate covalent bond with BF3.
A negatively charged species is called an anion, formed when an atom gains electrons. NH3 is not negatively charged; instead, it donates a lone pair of electrons, making this option incorrect.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct.A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B

questions.text: _ is also known as a dative bond.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: No, an ionic bond is not also known as a dative bond. An ionic bond is a type of chemical bond formed between a metal and a nonmetal. In an ionic bond, the metal donates one or more electrons to the nonmetal, which results in the formation of oppositely charged ions. The metal ion is positively charged, while the nonmetal ion is negatively charged. The oppositely charged ions are attracted to each other by electrostatic forces, which holds the molecule together.

No, covalent bond is not also known as a dative bond. A covalent bond is a type of chemical bond where two atoms share a pair of electrons. The electrons are shared equally between the two atoms. A dative bond is also a type of covalent bond, but it is a special type of covalent bond where one atom donates both electrons to the other.

A coordinate covalent bond is also known as a dative bond. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons are donated by the same atom. This type of bond is often formed between a Lewis acid and a Lewis base. A Lewis acid is an atom or molecule that can accept a pair of electrons. A Lewis base is an atom or molecule that can donate a pair of electrons. In a coordinate covalent bond, the Lewis acid donates a pair of electrons to the Lewis base. The Lewis base accepts the pair of electrons, and a covalent bond is formed.

No, metallic bond and dative bond are two different types of bonds. Metallic bond is a type of chemical bond that holds together metal atoms in a solid. It is formed by the delocalized electrons of the metal atoms. The delocalized electrons are free to move throughout the metal, which gives metals their characteristic properties, such as conductivity, malleability, and ductility. Dative bond (also called coordinate bond) is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons in the shared pair come from the same atom. It is formed when an atom with a lone pair of electrons donates that pair to an atom that is electron deficient.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct. A coordinate covalent bond is also known as a dative bond. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons are donated by the same atom. This type of bond is often formed between a Lewis acid and a Lewis base. A Lewis acid is an atom or molecule that can accept a pair of electrons. A Lewis base is an atom or molecule that can donate a pair of electrons. In a coordinate covalent bond, the Lewis acid donates a pair of electrons to the Lewis base. The Lewis base accepts the pair of electrons, and a covalent bond is formed.

questions.text: The distance between the nuclei of two atoms forming a covalent bond is called the bond length. Which of the following factors affect the bond lenght?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The greater the difference in electronegativity between the two atoms, the shorter the bond length. This is because the more electronegative atom will have a greater pull on the shared electrons, which will bring the two atoms closer together.
The larger the atoms, the longer the bond length. This is because the larger atoms have larger electron clouds, which means that the shared electrons are more spread out.
The greater the bond order, the shorter the bond length. This is because a higher bond order means that there are more shared electrons, which brings the two atoms closer together.
All above explanation.

questions.explanation: Option D is correct.The greater the difference in electronegativity between the two atoms, the shorter the bond length. This is because the more electronegative atom will have a greater pull on the shared electrons, which will bring the two atoms closer together. The larger the atoms, the longer the bond length. This is because the larger atoms have larger electron clouds, which means that the shared electrons are more spread out.The greater the bond order, the shorter the bond length. This is because a higher bond order means that there are more shared electrons, which brings the two atoms closer together.

questions.text: Ionic bond is produced after complete transfer of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The nucleus is not involved in the formation of ionic bonds; it remains intact within the atom.

An ionic bond is formed through the transfer of one or more valence electrons from one element to another, typically from a metal to a non-metal. This transfer results in the formation of positively and negatively charged ions that attract each other.
Neutrons do not participate in chemical bonding as they are neutral particles and remain within the nucleus.
Protons cannot be transferred during chemical bonding as they are part of the atom's nucleus and define the element.

questions.explanation: An ionic bond is formed through the transfer of one or more valence electrons from one element to another. The transfer of electrons mainly occurs from a metal to a non-metal.

questions.text: The ionization energy:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Increases from left to right in a period
options.explanation: Ionization energy increases from left to right in a period due to decrease in radius of the atom.

options.text: Increases from top to bottom in a group.
options.explanation: Ionization energy decreases in a group, due to increase in the radius of the atom.

options.text: Does not change in a period
options.explanation: Ionization energy changes in a period.

options.text: Does not change in a group
options.explanation: Ionization energy changes in a group.

questions.explanation: Ionization energy is increases from left to right in a period.

questions.text: Which covalent bond has the highest ionic character?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The covalent bond with the highest ionic character is HF. This is because fluorine has the highest electronegativity in the periodic table, and hydrogen has the lowest electronegativity. The difference in electronegativity between fluorine and hydrogen is 4.0, which is the largest difference of any of the choices.
HBr has an electronegativity difference of 2.8, which is lower than the electronegativity difference for HF and it has less ionic character.
HI has an electronegativity difference of 2.5, which is also lower than the electronegativity difference for HF.

H2O is a polar molecule, but it does not have a high ionic character. The electronegativity difference between hydrogen and oxygen is 1.2, which is not as large as the electronegativity difference for HF.

questions.explanation: The covalent bond with the highest ionic character is HF. This is because fluorine has the highest electronegativity of any element on the periodic table, and hydrogen has the lowest electronegativity. The difference in electronegativity between fluorine and hydrogen is 4.0, which is the largest difference of any of the choices.

questions.text: Maximum ionic character is exhibited by :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Strength of ionic character depends upon electronegativity difference.NaCl has a strong ionic bond ( 72% ionic character) but does not exhibit ionic character as much as CsF do (due to its greater E.N difference between Cs and F) ,hence this is an incorrect option.
Strength of ionic character depends upon electronegativity difference. CsF has greatest electronegativity difference of 3.19 and maximum ionic character of 92% ,hence this is the correct option.

Strength of ionic character depends upon electronegativity difference.KCl has a strong ionic bond but does not exhibit ionic character as much as CsF do (due to its greater E.N difference between Cs and F) ,hence this is an incorrect option.

Strength of ionic character depends upon electronegativity difference.HCl has a strong ionic bond but does not exhibit ionic character as much as CsF do (due to its greater E.N difference between Cs and F) ,hence this is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: Strength of ionic character depends upon electronegativity difference. CsF has greatest electronegativity difference of 3.19 and maximum ionic character of 92%.

questions.text: Type of bonding in Sodium (Na) is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Metallic bond' is a term used to describe the collective sharing of a sea of valence electrons between several positively charged metal ions. Metallic bonding is a type of chemical bonding that arises from the electrostatic attractive force between conduction electrons and positively charged metal ions. It may be described as the sharing of free electrons among a structure of positively charged ions. All the metal possesses this bond. Na being an alkali metal also possesses a metallic bond.

An ionic bond, also called an electrovalent bond, type of linkage formed from the electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions in a chemical compound. Such a bond forms when the valence (outermost) electrons of one atom are transferred permanently to another atom.

A covalent bond consists of the mutual sharing of one or more pairs of electrons between two atoms. These electrons are simultaneously attracted by the two atomic nuclei. A covalent bond forms when the difference between the electro-negativities of two atoms is too small for an electron transfer to occur to form ions. Hence, sodium cannot form covalent bond as sharing of electrons between two sodium atoms won’t complete their octet.

A coordinate bond is a type of alternate covalent bond that is formed by sharing of electron pair from a single atom. Both shared electrons are donated by the same atom. It is also called dative bond or dipolar bond. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The type of bonding in Sodium (Na) is metallic (Option A). 'Metallic bonding' implies the presence of an attractive force between delocalized electrons, initially present in the outermost orbit of the metal atoms, and positively charged metal cations. All metals possess this type of bond. An 'Ionic bond' is formed between a positively charged metal ion and a negatively charged nonmetal ion. A 'Covalent bond' is usually formed between two non-metals. Sodium does not possess a 'coordinate covalent bond' in between its atoms so, all the options are incorrect except Option A.

questions.text: Which of the following has perfect triangular structure :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: CO2 has a linear structure as it is AB2 type with two bonding pairs and zero lone pair, making it an incorrect option.
PH3 has a trigonal pyramidal structure as it is AB4 type with 3 bonding pairs and one lone pair, making it an incorrect option.
The nitrogen and oxygen atoms in NO are both diatomic molecules and are linear in shape, with a bond angle of 180 degrees, making it an incorrect option.
SO3 is AB3 type with three bond pairs and no lone pair, resulting in a perfect triangular structure, making it the correct option.

questions.explanation: SO3 is AB3 type with three bond pairs and no lone pair, resulting in a perfect triangular structure. This is why SO3 is the correct answer. CO2, PH3, and NO do not have perfect triangular structures due to their specific bond and lone pair arrangements.

questions.text: Which of the following species has lone pair of electrons on central atom?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The geometry of CH4 is tetrahedral. The central carbon (C) atom forms four single bonds with four hydrogen (H) atoms and has no lone pairs of electrons. Therefore, this option is incorrect as the central atom does not have any lone pairs.

The geometry of PCl5 is trigonal bipyramidal. The central phosphorus (P) atom forms five single bonds with five chlorine (Cl) atoms and has no lone pairs of electrons. Hence, this option is incorrect as the central atom does not have any lone pairs.

The geometry of NH4+ is tetrahedral. The central nitrogen (N) atom forms four single bonds with four hydrogen (H) atoms and has no lone pairs of electrons. Therefore, this option is incorrect because the central atom does not have any lone pairs.

The geometry of PCl3 is trigonal pyramidal. The central phosphorus (P) atom forms three single bonds with three chlorine (Cl) atoms and has one lone pair of electrons. This configuration results in a lone pair on the central atom, making this the correct option.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is PCl3. In PCl3, the central phosphorus atom forms three single bonds with chlorine atoms and has one lone pair of electrons, resulting in a trigonal pyramidal shape. In contrast, CH4, PCl5, and NH4+ all have central atoms with no lone pairs of electrons, leading to geometries of tetrahedral and trigonal bipyramidal, respectively.

questions.text: Demonstrate the correct use of articles and prepositions:I borrowed _ pencil from your pile of pencils and pens.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: This phrase I borrowed a pencil from your pile of pencils and pens is correct. The indefinite article "a" is used before "pencil" because it refers to one unspecified pencil among many in the pile. It indicates that the speaker took one pencil from the collection of pencils and pens.
Since ‘pencil’ starts with ‘p’ which has a consonant sound, we cannot use ‘an’ which is used with vowel sound, this option is incorrect.
Since, here, the pencil is a common noun, we cannot use ‘the’. This option is wrong.
No article is used when things are regarded in general and when the specific identity of a non-count noun is unknown. As explained, it is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: This phrase I borrowed a pencil from your pile of pencils and pens is correct. The indefinite article "a" is used before "pencil" because it refers to one unspecified pencil among many in the pile. It indicates that the speaker took one pencil from the collection of pencils and pens.

questions.text: Demonstrate the control of tenses and sentence structure:The horse _ across the fields.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Option A is a plural verb. Hence, it is an incorrect option.
Option B is a present participle. Hence, it is an incorrect option.

The subject of this question (horse) is singular and for subject-verb agreement, the verb must be singular.Option A is a plural verb, Option B is a present participle, Option C is a singular verb while Option D is also a present participle. Therefore, Option C is correct.
Option D is also a present participle. Hence, it is an incorrect option.

questions.text: Which part is incorrect in the following sentence?I found this bangle (A) while digging (B) in the backyard. (C) I don't know who it belonged to. (D)
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: This is correct because the subject “I” is followed by the verb “found” and the object “this bangle” in proper English word order. It forms a complete independent clause with a clear meaning.

This is correct because it introduces a participial phrase that modifies the subject “I,” indicating when or during what action the bangle was found. It does not need a separate subject because it refers to the main clause’s subject.
“In the backyard” is correct because it functions as a prepositional phrase specifying the location of the action “digging.” It clearly tells where the bangle was found.

options.text: I don't know who it belonged to
options.explanation: It should be 'to whom it belonged'. The sentence should read: I don't know to whom it belonged. When the relative pronoun is the object of the relative clause, we use whom instead of who. Note that, in this case, the preposition normally goes before whom.

questions.explanation: It should be ‘to whom it belonged’. The sentence should read: I don’t know to whom it belonged. When the relative pronoun is the object of the relative clause, we use whom instead of who. Note that, in this case, the preposition normally goes before whom.

questions.text: Demonstrate the correct use of subject-verb agreement:Plenty of mangoes and bananas _ available in this season.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Subject is plural, hence it is an incorrect option.

Since the sentence is in the present tense, 'was' and 'were' are incorrect options. The basic definition will be that plural nouns should use plural verbs. Singular nouns, for example, apple, mango, he, she, and so on, should be having singular verbs like 'is, has, and any verb +s. But for plural nouns like mangoes, bananas, etc, it should have plural verbs like 'are, have, and so on. In the given sentence, the nouns mangoes and bananas (along with the word 'plenty') are plurals. So the verb should be 'are'.
Since the sentence is in the present tense, 'was' and 'were' are incorrect options.
Since the sentence is in the present tense, 'was' and 'were' are incorrect options.

questions.explanation: Since the sentence is in the present tense, ‘was’ and ‘were’ are incorrect options.The basic definition will be, that plural nouns should use plural verbs. Singular nouns, for example, apple, mango, he, she, and so on, should be having singular verbs like ‘is, has, and any verb +s. But for plural nouns like mangoes, bananas, etc, it should have plural verbs like ‘are, have’, and so on.In the given sentence, the nouns mangoes and bananas (along with the word ‘plenty’) are plurals. So the verb should be ‘are’.

options.text: Samar bought an apple, an orange and an pear.

questions.text: The high school math department needs to appoint a new chairperson, which will be based on seniority. Ms. West has less seniority than Mr. Temple, but more than Ms. Brody. questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Mr. Temple has the most seniority, but he does not want the job. Next in line is Mr. Rhodes, who has more seniority than Ms. West or Ms. Brody.

questions.text: I. There has been a decline in the prices of personal computers. II. School-goings are expressing interest in learning how the computer operates.

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: Option I suggests that the decline in the prices of personal computers (Statement I) is the cause of school-goers expressing interest in learning how the computer operates (Statement II). It is an incorrect option.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect

The second statement states that students are interested in the operation of computers due to xyz reasons and it is in no way linked to the first statement

The second statement states that students are interested in the operation of computers due to xyz reasons and it is in no way linked to the first statement.

questions.text: Statement: The availability of imported fruits has increased in the indigenous market and so the demand for indigenous fruits has been decreased. I. To help the indigenous producers of fruits, the Government should impose high import duty on these fruits, even if these are not of good quality. II. The fruit vendors should stop selling imported fruits. So that the demand for indigenous fruits would be increased.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Both Statements I and II represent unfair means to cut competition.It is an incorrect option.

The ideas suggested in both Statements I and II represent unfair means to cut competition. The correct way would be to devise methods and techniques such that the indigenous producers could produce better quality fruits and make them available in the market at prices comparable with those of the imported ones. Hence, neither I nor II follows.

Imposing high import duty on imported fruits might make them more expensive, but it can also lead to negative consequences, such as an increase in the price of fruits for consumers and potential retaliation from trading partners. Additionally, imposing high import duties on low-quality fruits might not necessarily boost the demand for indigenous fruits if consumers still prefer the taste and quality of imported ones.

If fruit vendors stop selling imported fruits, it might indeed increase the demand for indigenous fruits. However, it could also limit consumer choices and preferences. Some consumers might prefer imported fruits for various reasons, such as taste, variety, or nutritional content.

questions.explanation: The ideas suggested in both Statements I and II represent unfair means to cut competition. The correct way would be to devise methods and techniques such that the indigenous producers could produce better quality fruits and make them available in the market at prices comparable with those of the imported ones. Hence, neither I nor II follows.
